response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said brings 15+ years of engineering breadth across distributed systems, full-stack development, and open-source tooling, but the resume shows no experience with experimentation platforms, A/B testing infrastructure, feature flagging, telemetry pipelines, growth measurement, or statistical analysis — the entire technical core of this Principal-level role. The candidate's career trajectory (Senior/Staff titles) also falls meaningfully short of the IC6 bar, and there is no evidence of cross-team architectural ownership or driving org-wide engineering strategy. Notably, the resume presents a significant integrity concern: the name 'Mohammed Al-Said' does not correspond to any of the listed contact details, which consistently point to a different individual (szabobogdan), raising questions about submission authenticity.
strengths: Polyglot engineer with demonstrated hands-on proficiency across C, C++, JavaScript/TypeScript, Java, and C#, covering the full language set listed in required qualifications
Real distributed systems exposure at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O, caching, and high-performance databases
Active community contributor through conference talks, public workshops, and open-source projects, suggesting communication and knowledge-sharing capacity
concerns: No experimentation platform experience whatsoever: zero mention of A/B testing, feature flagging, experiment assignment, rollout automation, or bias/guardrail systems — the primary technical domain of this role
No evidence of telemetry infrastructure, metrics pipelines, or statistical/data analysis work, all of which are listed as required qualifications

Career seniority (self-described as Senior/Staff) does not reach the Principal/IC6 bar, and there is no record of owning multi-team technical initiatives or translating ambiguous business goals into measurable engineering systems at scale

key_factors.factor: Absence of experimentation and growth systems experience
Seniority gap relative to Principal/IC6 expectations
Distributed systems and polyglot engineering background
